title: About me - Gerald Jones
meta_description: Member of Parliament for Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare
text_excerpt: About me - Gerald Jones Skip to main content About Privacy Policy Social Media Unacceptable Behaviour Policy Monthly Newsletters Newsletter sign up Contact me Get in touch Advice Surgeries Meet Gerald Free Tour of Parliament Tours of the Elizabeth Tower and Big Ben Educational Visits to the UK Parliament Emergency contacts Campaigns Bryn Pica Survey 2026 Barber Survery Cost of Living Support More Search Search the website Search Menu About me Gerald Jones MP Gerald Jones – MP for Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare. A champion for our valleys in Parliament. I was first elected to the House of Commons in May 2015, for the then constituency of Merthyr Tydfil & Rhymney. I was re-elected in 2017 and 2019. Following changes to the parliamentary boundaries, I was elected for the new constituency of Merthyr Tydfil & Aberdare in July 2024. I am proud to serve in Keir Starmer’s government as a whip. I was born and raised in New Tredegar, where my interest in community development and politics began as a teenager during the 1984 Miner’s Strike. Prior to being elected as a MP, I worked with community and voluntary groups for a County Voluntary Council. I was also a local councillor and deputy leader of Caerphilly County Borough Council. As Member of Parliament, my focus is very much on the needs of our communities. I am enthusiastic advocate for workers’ rights, environmental sustainability, and equality for all. All my efforts in parliament are driven by a desire to create positive and lasting change and improve the quality of life for people across Merthyr Tydfil and Aberdare. I hold regular advice surgeries, attend, and organise local events, and I am always ready to listen to the concerns of my constituents. Please feel free to get in touch if you feel I can help . Contact details Crownford House, Swan Street, Merthyr Tydfil CF47 8EU and First Floor, 27 a&b, High Street, Aberdare, CF44 7AA.
